development.title: Development
development.description: Want to contribute to Board Games Tracker? Here's how to set up a local development environment.
development.step1Description: Clone the repository and install the dependencies:
development.step2Description: Set up the database and run the application:
development.step3Description: When starting the application it will first start the API process, when successfull it will open a new command window to run the UI. Browse to http://localhost:5443 to access the application. Swagger UI will be avaliable at http://localhost:6554
development.step4Description: For the database, you will need to set up an empty database. On startup, the application itself will run migrations to setup the database.

translations.title: Translations
translations.description: Board Games Tracker supports multiple languages. You can help translate the application into your language with the help of Crowdin tooling.
translations.howToDescription: Navigate to the Crowdin project for Board Games Tracker and follow the instructions to contribute translations. If your language is not yet listed, you can send me a message on Crowdin or create a Feature request on GitHub and I will add the new language asap.

bugsFeatures.title: Bugs / Features
bugsFeatures.description: Found a bug or have a feature request? Feel free to open an issue on GitHub so I can get it fixed asap. I created some usefull templates to report a bug or feature request. Screenshots and logs are always more then welcom when logging a bug.

logging.title: Logging
logging.description: Board Games Tracker uses structured logging to help you (and me) debug issues. To change the log level, you need to stop the application, set the LOGLEVEL environment variable to the desired level, and then restart the application. The available log levels are debug, info, warn, and error. The default log level is warn.
logging.viewingDescription: You can view the application logs using the following docker command
logging.sentry1Description: Board Games Tracker is integrated with Sentry for error tracking. You can enable or disable this feature by setting the STATISTICS_ENABLED environment variable. When true, any unhandled exceptions will be sent to Sentry along with relevant context and metadata to help diagnose and fix issues quickly. No IP, servername, hardware info is send to Sentry at anytime. Only stack traces and spans.
logging.sentry2Description: By enabling this, you can help improve the application by providing valuable insights into any errors that occur. For privacy reasons this feature is disabled by default.
